summary: Comprehensive Playwright testing of HackerWare static frontend game. 4/5 mini-games work, 2 are broken due to a shared root cause (area id rename breaks localRect/spawnBurst helpers). Start/intro/HUD/timer/round/feedback/game-over/restart/randomization/sounds all work. NO BACKEND TESTING (static app).

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Clicking the cyan phish-line throws 'Cannot read properties of null (reading getBoundingClientRect)' so onWin is never scheduled and the player can NEVER win this mini-game. Root cause: mount() does `area.id = 'phish-area'`, but the helper `localRect(el)` (called immediately on click via `const r = localRect(line)`) does `document.getElementById('game-area').getBoundingClientRect()` which is null. The exception aborts the handler before `setTimeout(onWin, 600)` is reached. Verified by clicking line in fresh page state - line and bait animate (cut/dropped) but feedback screen never appears.

CRITICAL: Same root cause as Phishing. mount() does `area.id = 'meme-area'`, so when a bad bubble is clicked, `spawnBurst(...)` calls `document.getElementById('game-area')` which is null, then `ga.appendChild(p)` throws. The win logic (`badRemaining--; if (badRemaining<=0) onWin`) sits AFTER spawnBurst inside the same handler, so it never runs. Effectively impossible to win Meme Shield.

Spec says `window.SoundSynth`, `window.Game`, `window.MiniGames` must be 'callable and existing'. They are declared with `const` so they are NOT properties of window (window.SoundSynth === undefined). They are accessible lexically inside page-context expressions, but not via window.* — fails the literal acceptance criterion. `window.nextRound` and `window.startNewGame` ARE on window because they are function declarations.

integration_issues.issue: Click on line cuts visually but never advances to feedback screen
Clicking bad bubble never reduces badRemaining counter or triggers onWin

issues: Instruction text near top (CLICK THE GLOWING CYAN LINE TO SNIP IT!) visually overlaps the TIME label/bar in the HUD at 1920x800 viewport. Hard to read both.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): In Phishing mini-game mount, do NOT rename `area.id = 'phish-area'`. Instead toggle a CSS class on #game-area (e.g. `area.classList.add('phish-mode')`) and add CSS `#game-area.phish-mode { cursor: none; }`. Update cleanup to remove the class. Same fix for Meme Shield ('meme-area'). Alternative: change `localRect` and `spawnBurst` to accept an area element or look it up by querying the active screen rather than hard-coded id.
FIX (CRITICAL): Verify all mini-games can be WON end-to-end after the area-id fix.

FIX (MEDIUM): Explicitly expose dev hooks on window: `window.SoundSynth = SoundSynth; window.Game = Game; window.MiniGames = MiniGames; window.Screens = Screens; window.Timer = Timer; window.renderLives = renderLives;` so the acceptance criterion 'window.SoundSynth callable' is satisfied, and the agent-to-agent forced-mini-game console snippet (Game.lives=3; Game.miniGameQueue=[MiniGames[N]]; nextRound();) works from external contexts.
FIX (MINOR): Move the per-mini-game instruction text below the HUD (currently ~y=10px which sits behind the TIME label/bar).
critical_code_review_comments: Single 1600+ line HTML file is hard to maintain. Consider extracting JS modules.
`localRect()` and `spawnBurst()` hard-code `document.getElementById('game-area')` but Phishing/Meme renaming the area id silently breaks them — fragile coupling. Pass the area element explicitly.

`startMiniGame` never cleans up the PREVIOUSLY active mini-game's cleanup() — only finish() does. If `nextRound()` is called externally before the previous round naturally ended, the prior mini-game's setIntervals/event listeners may leak. The auto-advance setTimeout(3200) in showFeedback also races with click-to-continue (the `advanced` flag mitigates this, but the setTimeout is never cleared even after advance ran).

Game.miniGameQueue defaults to all 5 then reshuffles — fine. But round counter formula `Game.roundsWon + (3 - Game.lives) + 1` will compute incorrectly if both lives lost and rounds won don't sum cleanly (it does in normal play but it's fragile).
Top-level `const` declarations are not attached to window — breaks documented dev hook API.
Tip text is identical for win and lose for every mini-game — consider differentiating to reinforce learning on losses.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (3/5 mini-games work end-to-end: Password Puzzle, Fake News Detective, Inbox Cleaner; Phishing & Meme Shield broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 5 mini-games have data-testid hooks. To force-test a mini-game: `Game.lives=3; Game.miniGameQueue=[MiniGames[N]]; renderLives(); Screens.hud(true); nextRound();` then wait for screen-game.active (handles the 2s intro). For drag tests use mouse.down/move/up at piece center → slot center (snap threshold 70px in local 960-wide coords; bounding_box gives real screen coords which scale automatically). After main agent fixes the area-id bug, retest Phishing (click cyan line, click bait separately) and Meme Shield (click each bad red bubble). Inbox Cleaner test was not exercised end-to-end here — also verify drag-virus-to-INBOX triggers instant lose. Console error 'Cannot read properties of null (reading getBoundingClientRect)' is the smoking gun for the area-id bug.
rca_of_the_issue: Phishing and Meme Shield mini-games set `area.id = 'phish-area'` / `'meme-area'` to apply custom cursor CSS (rules `#phish-area { cursor: none; }` and `#meme-area { cursor: none; }`). The helper functions `localRect()` (line 920) and `spawnBurst()` (line 943) hard-code `document.getElementById('game-area')`. After the rename, getElementById returns null. localRect immediately calls `.getBoundingClientRect()` on null → TypeError. In Phishing's cutHandler this exception fires before `setTimeout(onWin, 600)` is reached, so the round never resolves. In Meme Shield, spawnBurst's `ga.appendChild(p)` throws, and the badRemaining decrement / onWin check that come after never execute. Reproduction: open /hackerware.html, click PLAY, force Game.miniGameQueue=[MiniGames[4]]; nextRound(); after intro, click the cyan line — feedback screen never appears, pageerror logged. Mitigation options: (1) don't rename the area id; apply cursor styling via classList instead, with CSS like `#game-area.phish-mode { cursor: none; }`; (2) refactor `localRect` and `spawnBurst` to take the area element as a parameter or to look it up via `.screen.active .game-area` selector.
